metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Midnight Hunt is a faster-paced Limited format with a lower mana curve than typical sets. The format features robust common removal, particularly in black, which significantly impacts deckbuilding decisions. Powerful rares exist but the set maintains a relatively balanced power level between rarities due to efficient answers.

color_notes.{U} Blue: Best color overall. Organ Hoarder is among the strongest commons. Deep playable support across all archetypes.
color_notes.{B} Black: Best removal density at common; devastating against werewolves.
color_notes.{W} White: Solid creatures and removal; supports multiple archetypes.
color_notes.{R} Red: Weakest color. Underperforming werewolves and situational combat tricks become dead draws when behind.
color_notes.{G} Green: Lacks efficient common removal; relies on combat tricks and uncommons.

key_gameplay_notes: Stick to two colors in most cases; splash single-color bombs strategically.
Lower average mana curve than typical; flashback provides late-game utility.
Faster than average sets but control-focused decks perform equally.
Blue pairs exceptionally well across all color pairs; red combinations are generally weakest.
Substantial sideboard depth available — swap removal, upgrade creatures, and add hate cards.

mechanics.name: Day / Night and Daybound / Nightbound
mechanics.summary: Persistent cycle tracked across the game. Daybound creatures enter flipped at night. Day becomes night when a player doesn't cast spells on their turn; night becomes day when a player casts two or more spells on their turn.
mechanics.performance: Anchors werewolves; passive global state for everyone.

mechanics.name: Disturb
mechanics.summary: Cast creatures with disturb from the graveyard in transformed form as flying spirits. Exclusive to Azorius with one exception.

key_examples.name: Lunarch Veteran
key_examples.type_line: Creature — Human Cleric // Creature — Spirit Cleric

key_examples.name: Mourning Patrol
key_examples.type_line: Creature — Human Soldier // Creature — Spirit Soldier

mechanics.name: Coven
mechanics.summary: Reward mechanic requiring three or more creatures with different power values. Exclusive to Selesnya.

mechanics.name: Decayed
mechanics.summary: Keyword on zombie tokens. Creatures with decayed can't block and sacrifice after attacking. Fairly costed in reality.

mechanics.name: Flashback
mechanics.summary: Returning mechanic — cast from graveyard by paying flashback cost. Appears at common in every color and uncommon in each pair.
mechanics.performance: Primary mana flood outlet; limited graveyard hate makes it reliable.

mechanics.name: Investigate
mechanics.summary: Returning mechanic — create Clue tokens. Mana sink and card draw outlet.

mechanics.name: Curse Cycle
mechanics.summary: Four rare player-targeting auras. Only Curse of Leeches rates as playable; others are essentially unplayable.

key_examples.name: Curse of Leeches
key_examples.type_line: Enchantment — Aura Curse // Creature — Leech Horror

tier_1.name: Azorius Spirits
tier_1.strategy: Grindy archetype leveraging disturb creatures and flying pressure. Ground-based curve filler becomes aerial threats post-board.

key_cards.name: Dennick, Pious Apprentice
key_cards.type_line: Legendary Creature — Human Soldier // Legendary Creature — Spirit Soldier

key_cards.name: Lunarch Veteran
key_cards.type_line: Creature — Human Cleric // Creature — Spirit Cleric

key_cards.name: Mourning Patrol
key_cards.type_line: Creature — Human Soldier // Creature — Spirit Soldier

tier_1.name: Dimir Zombies
tier_1.strategy: Accumulates decayed tokens through gameplay, sacrificing for value payoffs. Less dependent on high-rarity cards.

tier_1.name: Simic Flashback
tier_1.strategy: Ranges from pure durdle to efficient creature piles. Self-mill fuels flashback and graveyard payoffs. Most splash-friendly pair.

tier_2.name: Selesnya Coven / Humans
tier_2.strategy: Aggressive creature-heavy deck maximizing coven synergies. Humans theme is present but lacks common payoffs.

tier_2.name: Orzhov Sacrifice
tier_2.strategy: Midrange sacrificial synergy deck. Accumulates small creatures and tokens, sacrificing for incremental value.

tier_2.name: Rakdos Vampires
tier_2.strategy: Aggressive strategy leveraging "life loss matters" payoffs. Pressures opponent while enabling recursive effects.

tier_3.name: Boros Day/Night Aggro
tier_3.strategy: Most aggressive archetype with typical Boros limitations (card disadvantage, weak late game). Day/Night provides a unique angle.

tier_3.name: Golgari Morbid
tier_3.strategy: Clunky midrange deck with self-mill and morbid synergies. Simic is generally superior at a similar plan.

tier_3.name: Izzet Spells
tier_3.strategy: Spell-focused tempo or control variant. Clunky but functional with proper construction.

{U} Blue.name: Organ Hoarder
{U} Blue.note: Arguably the best common in the set.

general_tips: Stick to two colors in most cases; splash single-color bombs strategically.
Lower average mana curve than typical sets — aggression scales well.
Flashback provides late-game utility despite the low curve.
Blue is the universal pair-up; red is the worst pair-up.
Sideboard aggressively in BO3 with removal swaps and hate inclusions.
